evidence_quotes.quote: Primarily games, that are single player but require constant online connection, and game library account to "enhance" the experience.
Micro transactions in some games piling up to thousand of euros IF someone wants to get all of the unlock able items.
Without laws companies will sell unfinished games, with micro transactions, with extra content that should have been part of the base game.
These paid digital products are targeted at children and gambling addicts.
A printer should not have a subscription as it is a tool.
No sane person would pay monthly for a hammer to use
Take-downs that happen because someone's opinion about said content or product that does not align the values of CEOs, shareholders or other executives.
Just because a game has strong, graphic, or adult content it should not be able to be removed from the platforms by third-parties.

It is solely the developer's and the platform's choice to allow the content and partners of that platform should not be able to threaten with denial of service or breaking the contract just because they do not like something that the other party does or allows.

Sell licenses to books that will not be readable after missing payment, cars that will stop moving if an invoice is due, and this will continue until the very air we breathe will be locked behind a monthly paywall to access.

paragraphs.text: Current state of online fairness is at least sad. Companies sell digital products as licenses and no way to still use these online-only products after the "live service" ended. Primarily games, that are single player but require constant online connection, and game library account to "enhance" the experience. Micro transactions in some games piling up to thousand of euros IF someone wants to get all of the unlock able items. Not to mention the "option" to "earn" 10euros worth of digital accessories after heavily investing time in some games that can reach up to weeks or months. These paid digital products are targeted at children and gambling addicts. Companies that excessively implement micro-transactions take money away from the less fortunate for parts of something that has been already purchased. Take-downs that happen because someone's opinion about said content or product that does not align the values of CEOs, shareholders or other executives. Just because a game has strong, graphic, or adult content it should not be able to be removed from the platforms by third-parties. It is solely the developer's and the platform's choice to allow the content and partners of that platform should not be able to threaten with denial of service or breaking the contract just because they do not like something that the other party does or allows. These problems even extend to physical goods like printers or smart electronics and cars. A printer should not have a subscription as it is a tool. No sane person would pay monthly for a hammer to use, and no company should be able to disable the hammer because of the nail the user buys for it. The fact that these problems not even theoretical but companies actively exploit and is considered a norm in the digital industry is embodies dystopia. Without laws companies will sell unfinished games, with micro transactions, with extra content that should have been part of the base game. Sell licenses to books that will not be readable after missing payment, cars that will stop moving if an invoice is due, and this will continue until the very air we breathe will be locked behind a monthly paywall to access. I call for digital fairness and I am in full support of the idea to put an end to this never ending profits-before-products worldview.
